questions.question: What is the primary characteristic that distinguishes a combinational logic circuit from a sequential logic circuit?
options: Combinational circuits generate output signals that depend only on the current input signals.
Sequential circuits always include a clock signal, while combinational circuits never do.
Combinational circuits are built using only NAND gates, whereas sequential circuits use NOR gates.
Sequential circuits have faster propagation delays than combinational circuits.
Combinational circuits can store data, while sequential circuits cannot.

questions.question: In the digital IC design flow, what is the primary purpose of performing "post-layout simulation"?
options: To generate the initial Register Transfer Level (RTL) code from high-level specifications.
To optimize the power delivery network before transistor placement.
To verify the functional correctness of the design by incorporating extracted parasitic effects from the physical layout.
To convert the gate-level netlist into a hardware description language (HDL).
To determine the optimal placement of standard cells to minimize chip area.

questions.question: What is the overarching goal of techniques like pipelining, superscalar execution, and out-of-order execution in modern CPU architectures?
options: To reduce the thermal design power (TDP) of the processor.
To decrease the clock frequency while maintaining the same performance.
To increase Instruction Level Parallelism (ILP) by executing multiple instructions concurrently or overlapping their execution stages.
To enhance the accuracy of branch prediction algorithms.
To simplify the instruction set architecture (ISA) for easier compiler development.

questions.question: Beyond the Design Under Test (DUT), which of the following components is most critical for a comprehensive functional verification testbench in digital IC design?
options: A simple text file listing all input signals and their expected values.
A power management unit (PMU) for dynamic voltage scaling.
A scoreboard or checker module to compare actual outputs with expected outputs.
A custom hardware accelerator for faster simulation.
A dedicated physical design rule checker.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of implementing a multi-level cache hierarchy (e.g., L1, L2, L3) in a computer processor?
options: To eliminate the need for main system memory (DRAM).
To improve overall memory access speed by providing increasingly larger and slower caches further from the CPU.
To reduce the complexity of the CPU's instruction decoder.
To allow for direct memory access (DMA) without CPU intervention.
To completely prevent cache coherence issues in multi-core systems.
